# Topic Blurbs — LLM Edition

1) International experience → resilience and adaptability  [00:40–02:39]
A portable crucible: moving across countries forces rapid learning, social range, and grit. The compounding effect becomes an unfair advantage for PMs making ambiguous calls.

2) Soft skills as “base athleticism”  [07:21–08:57]
Skills stick when the substrate is strong: learn‑how‑to‑learn, communicate precisely, build trust quickly. Tooling rides on top of this base.

3) AI as an amplifier of judgment  [09:45–11:35]
The lift comes from knowing when you’re at the edge of context and how to structure a prompt‑check‑refine loop. The same model can be a blunt stick or a scalpel.

4) PM‑first go‑to‑market  [22:52–24:41]
Start where we have signal: product managers. On the buyer side, Seed–Series A–C teams who need one or two excellent PMs with minimal noise.

5) Engagement with Shujaat  [31:15–36:18]
Offer scoped tiers; ship a small success. Pick: a weekly myth‑busting case series or a “base athleticism” micro‑series. Decide success metrics up front.
